original_abstract: Integrating the philosophy of Christian Religious Education (PAK) into the curriculum of theological colleges is not only academically sound but spiritually essential. Often misunderstood as a secular or confusing discipline, philosophy, when viewed as the pursuit of wisdom, finds its highest expression in alignment with Biblical truth. This study explores the vital role of PAK philosophy in shaping the intellectual and spiritual maturity of theology students, enabling them to cultivate wise thoughts, feelings, and actions. By positioning the Holy Scriptures as the ultimate standard for philosophical inquiry, students can reconcile the relationship between faith and reason. The study concludes that specialized learning in this field is fundamental for building up both the individual student and the wider community, ensuring that theological practice is grounded in a robust, Christ-centered worldview.
humanized_abstract: The integration of PAK into the curricula of theological colleges is not only academically sound but also spiritually relevant. Philosophy as the pursuit of wisdom is also uniquely aligned with the biblical world view. Therefore, this research aims to investigate the impact that the philosophy component of PAK has on the intellectual and spiritual growth of students at theological colleges so that they may attain wise thinking, feeling and behaviour. In so doing the study also aims to illustrate that the holy scriptures may serve as the normative standard for philosophical investigations of faith and reason, thereby redressing the aparent dichotomy between these two concepts. The research conclusively affirms that philosophical study of any kind is vital for the intellectual, spiritual and practical growth of students in theological colleges and thus for the entire body of Christ.

The research conclusively affirms that philosophical study of any kind is vital for the intellectual, spiritual and practical growth of students in theological colleges and thus for the entire body of Christ.
